content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CH Al RM AN
FAIR
POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
April 16,
1996
Francine Rice—Laabs
President
National Education Association
— Jurupa
9227 Orco Parkway,
Suite
E
Riverside,
California
92509
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-96—l10
Dear Ms.
Rice—Laabs:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
permissible use of campaign funds provisions of the Political
Reform Act
(the “Act”) •1
QUESTION
May you use funds from your established PAC for the purpose
of supporting a campaign to recall the current school board
members in your district prior to the actual recall election in
November of 1996?
CONCLUSION
Yes.
You may use funds from your established recipient
committee to make expenditures in connection with a recall
election involving school board members in your district.
FACTS
You are currently the treasurer of a recipient committee
controlled by the National Education Association.
The committee
was formed,
as stated in the committee’s statement of
organization,
as a general purpose committee to “serve as a
voluntary funding structure to support or oppose State and local
candidates.”
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000—18995.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—96—1lO
Page
2
The committee would like to support a campaign to recall the
current school board members in your district.
The election will
be held in November of 1996.
ANALYSIS
Section 89512.5 controls the use of funds by a committee not
controlled by a candidate.
Generally,
any committee expenditure
reasonably related to a political purpose is permitted.
If an
expenditure confers a substantial personal benefit on an
individual who has authority to approve an expenditure of campaign
funds held by the couuuittee, then such an expenditure must be
directly related to the political purpose of the committee.
You are the treasurer of a general purpose recipient
committee.
The committee is controlled by the National Education
Association and would like to make expenditures to support a
campaign to recall the current school board members in your area.
Expenditures made by a recipient committee controlled by an
education organization to participate in the recall of school
board members appears to be directly related to a political,
legislative or governmental purpose.
Accordingly, this
expenditure of committee funds is permitted under the Act.
2
If you have any further questions, please feel free to
contact me at
(916)
322—5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Liane Randolph
Counsel,
Legal Division
SGC:LR: ak
2
This advice is limited to the provisions of the Act and does
not address any state or federal, taxation issues that may arise
from such expenditures.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May you use funds from your established PAC for the purpose
of supporting a campaign to recall the current school board
members in your district prior to the actual recall election in
November of 1996?

CONCLUSION: Yes.
You may use funds from your established recipient
committee to make expenditures in connection with a recall
election involving school board members in your district.
